page: Microsoft Azure D12 is a D-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8171M processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 1.0 thread per core, a 35 MB L3 cache, and support for AVX and AVX-512F instruction sets. It is equipped with 28.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 205 GB of local storage.

Benchmark results indicate that the D12 server exhibits weak to poor CPU performance. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests place the processor in the bottom 25 percent, while single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression fall into the bottom 10 percent. However, memory subsystem performance is more resilient, with memory bandwidth tests and PassMark memory scores ranking in the middle 50 percent. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve average performance tiers, processing 5.98 million operations per second on the Redis benchmark.

The primary tradeoff of the Azure D12 instance is its low computational throughput relative to its memory capacity. While the 28.0 GB memory allocation provides a high memory-to-core ratio, the older Intel Xeon 8171M processor limits its utility for CPU-intensive tasks. This hardware profile is best suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU performance, such as caching layers, moderate database operations, or lightweight web serving.
description: The Microsoft Azure D12 is a D-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance is equipped with 28.0 GB of RAM and 205 GB of local storage. Benchmark data shows weak CPU performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Conversely, memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, with Redis SET operations reaching 5.98 million operations per second. Given the high memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB per core alongside constrained CPU throughput, the D12 is best suited for memory-reliant workloads with moderate processing requirements, such as caching layers, database operations, and basic web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D12 is a D-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M cores, 28.0 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage, offering balanced memory performance for moderate database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D12 is a 4-vCPU virtual machine with 28.0 GB of RAM and 205 GB of local storage, suited for moderate database and caching workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine combining 28 GB of RAM and local storage for memory-focused workloads with moderate computational demands.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8171M processor with 4 dedicated physical cores at 2.6 GHz
28.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core
Average-tier memory bandwidth and database operation performance
Suited for caching layers, moderate database tasks, and web serving
